title: Aris pack (v1)
description: The data contract a member provides to teach Aris a domain. Pure data — no engine details. v1 adds optional sections (aliases, polarity, thresholds, focus, out_of_domain, dates, watchdog) that teach Aris more of how your people talk and what 'doesn't add up' — all still plain data. v0 packs remain valid.

record_schema.description: The fields your records carry and their kind. Use 'date' for an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD).

vocab.description: How your users' words map to fields and answer kinds.

agg.description: Words your users say for a kind of answer.

metric.description: Words your users say for a numeric field (values must be keys of record_schema).

aliases.description: Words your users say that mean a specific VALUE in one of your fields — e.g. 'fast food' means the industry 'quick service', 'the bank' means a lender. Keyed by field name; each maps an everyday phrase to a value (or a distinctive part of a value) that appears in your records.

polarity.description: Idioms that mean 'the good end' or 'the bad end' of a number — e.g. 'bleeding money' means the lowest margin, 'safest loans' means the lowest loan-to-value. Each entry lists the words and which direction, on which field, they point.

thresholds.description: Named filters your users say — 'high-risk' means loan-to-value over 80, 'profitable' means margin over 0. Aris counts and filters the records that meet them, and follow-ups can stack on top.

focus.description: Which field answers a question word — 'where' -> location, 'who' -> the party, 'when' -> the date. Keyed by the question word; the value is the field(s) that answer it.

out_of_domain.description: Words that signal a question your records can't answer — 'ceo', 'weather', 'forecast', 'credit score'. When one appears, Aris says 'no record' instead of guessing on an incidental name match.

field.description: The record_schema field (kind 'date') that time questions filter on.

watchdog.description: Consistency rules Aris checks across your records and signs. A record that breaks one is flagged with YOUR message and the reason — the rest stay silent (a clean bill). All declarative: no code.

message.description: Plain-English reason shown when a record breaks this rule.

expr.description: For kind 'formula': an arithmetic relationship among your fields using + - * / ( ) and one '==', e.g. 'margin_pct == (contract - build_cost) / contract * 100'. Aris flags records where the two sides differ by more than 'tolerance'.

before.description: For kind 'order': a date/step field that must not come after 'after'.

k.description: For kind 'outlier': how many spreads out counts as an outlier (e.g. 3).

compute.description: How numbers read back to your users.

cite.description: How a matched record reads back as plain English.
